name: 24_Diabetes_Prediction_LogisticRegression_PimaIndians_ML
query: Configura un proyecto de predicción de diabetes utilizando un modelo de Regresión Logística y el conjunto de datos Pima Indians Diabetes. Realiza el escalado de características y la estandarización de datos en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa validación cruzada para evaluar el modelo en `src/train.py`, y guarda la puntuación de precisión en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Genera y guarda la curva ROC en `results/figures/roc_curve.png`. Crea un panel interactivo usando Tableau o Power BI para mostrar el rendimiento del modelo y resaltar características importantes. Asegúrate de que el panel sea fácil de usar y documenta los pasos de procesamiento del conjunto de datos y creación de visualizaciones. Durante el desarrollo, el sistema debe gestionar automáticamente la apertura y cierre de Tableau o Power BI para evitar bloqueos innecesarios.

requirements.criteria: Se utiliza el conjunto de datos "Pima Indians Diabetes".
El escalado de características y la estandarización de datos se implementan en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se implementa un modelo de "Regresión Logística" en `src/model.py`.
Se utiliza validación cruzada para evaluar el modelo en `src/train.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se guarda en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.
La curva ROC se genera y guarda como `results/figures/roc_curve.png`.
Se crea un panel de visualización interactivo usando "Tableau" o "Power BI" para mostrar el rendimiento del modelo y características importantes.

preferences.criteria: El panel debe permitir a los usuarios explorar diferentes aspectos del rendimiento del modelo y entender qué características contribuyen más a las predicciones.
El panel debe mostrar claramente cómo se procesó el conjunto de datos y cómo se crearon las visualizaciones.
Durante el desarrollo, el sistema debe abrir y cerrar automáticamente "Tableau" o "Power BI" según sea necesario para evitar largos períodos de bloqueo o inactividad.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza el conjunto de datos "Pima Indians Diabetes".

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The code files (`data_loader.py`, `model.py`, `train.py`) are all minimal placeholder implementations that contain no actual dataset loading logic. There is no reference to the "Pima Indians Diabetes" dataset, no loading of any CSV file (e.g., `diabetes.csv` or `pima-indians-diabetes.csv`), and no data processing whatsoever. The `load_dataset()` function in `data_loader.py` simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", ...}` without reading any real data. The trajectory evidence also confirms no dataset loading was observed during execution.

judge_stats.criteria: El escalado de características y la estandarización de datos se implementan en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a placeholder/stub implementation. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual data loading, and `build_transforms()` returns a static list of strings `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` with no real feature scaling or standardization logic. There is no use of libraries like `sklearn.preprocessing.StandardScaler`, `MinMaxScaler`, or any equivalent mechanism that would actually perform feature scaling or data standardization on the Pima Indians dataset.

judge_stats.criteria: Se implementa un modelo de "Regresión Logística" en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement a Logistic Regression model. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns a generic `"model placeholder"` string. There is no actual import or instantiation of any Logistic Regression class (e.g., `from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression`), no model training logic, and no real implementation of the required algorithm. The file appears to be a minimal fake implementation designed to superficially reference the requirement without actually fulfilling it.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza validación cruzada para evaluar el modelo en `src/train.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/train.py` file contains only a minimal stub implementation that does not perform any actual machine learning. The `train()` function simply iterates over a range and accumulates a sum — there is no dataset loading, no logistic regression model, and critically, no cross-validation (e.g., `cross_val_score`, `KFold`, `StratifiedKFold`, or any similar mechanism). The file explicitly labels itself as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and only creates placeholder artifact files. No cross-validation is implemented anywhere in the code.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se guarda en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` exists in the project structure, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` — a placeholder string rather than an actual accuracy score value. This indicates the file was not properly populated with a real computed accuracy score from model evaluation. The trajectory evidence also confirms no relevant execution related to saving an accuracy score was found.

judge_stats.criteria: La curva ROC se genera y guarda como `results/figures/roc_curve.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/roc_curve.png` exists in the project structure, as confirmed by the workspace tree which explicitly lists `results\figures\roc_curve.png`. The file was generated and saved at the expected path. The error shown in the file content is merely a rendering/reading error from the evaluation tool (related to image format detection during base64 encoding), not an indication that the file is missing or corrupt — the file itself is present in the correct location.

judge_stats.criteria: Se crea un panel de visualización interactivo usando "Tableau" o "Power BI" para mostrar el rendimiento del modelo y características importantes.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The project contains only three Python source files (`data_loader.py`, `model.py`, `train.py`) and two result artifacts (`accuracy_score.txt`, `roc_curve.png`). There is no evidence of any Tableau (`.twb`, `.twbx`) or Power BI (`.pbix`, `.pbit`) files in the workspace structure. The code files are minimal placeholder implementations with no integration or export to either of these visualization platforms. The trajectory evidence also explicitly confirms that no actions related to Tableau or Power BI were performed during project execution.
